topic: Weak-quotient inference phase for proximal bridge target uniqueness. Pre-anchor check: closest anchors are the Zhang-Li-Miao-Tchetgen Tchetgen null-annihilator criterion for nonunique bridges, proximal AIPW bridge estimation papers, and weak-identification/singular-matrix inference. Why non-trivial? The target is not the already-solved algebraic quotient criterion and not another completeness threshold: require a fixed-rank finite negative-control model where the bridge operator and target loading are estimated, and derive a local-to-nullspace phase theorem for the plug-in quotient diagnostic. The nonroutine object is a cone-angle certificate: when the target-loading distance to the estimated bridge nullspace is separated from zero, ordinary Gaussian inference is valid; when it is n^{-1/2}-local, the diagnostic has a nonstandard projected-Gaussian/chi-bar-square law and naive quotient-identification decisions have nonvanishing size distortion. Why promising? This is estimator geometry around a published identification criterion, with a two-matrix hand witness and explicit phase boundary, not LP duality or support coverage. If the delta reduces to standard singular-value perturbation, generic weak-IV analogies, path derivatives, or rephrasing the Zhang nullspace iff, pivot or accept field-tier.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the local-to-nullspace sampling law for a plug-in proximal quotient/null-annihilator certificate when both the finite bridge operator and the target loading are estimated?

source_refs.claim: Nonunique proximal bridge solutions complicate estimation and inference; root-n estimability of a linear functional is tied to a dual bridge and additional regularity.
source_refs.author: Zhang, Li, Miao, Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: In proximal causal inference and related inverse problems, linear functionals can be root-n estimable even when nuisance functions are weakly identified or nonunique.
source_refs.author: Bennett, Kallus, Mao, Newey, Syrgkanis, Uehara

source_refs.claim: Estimated-matrix rank tests can be built from transformed singular values, but they target rank rather than target-loading distance to a nullspace.
source_refs.author: Kleibergen, Paap

source_refs.excerpt: derive fixed-rank asymptotic behavior and diagnostics for near-nullspace violations
Future proximal topics should build on that result, for example by studying inference

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published proximal results establish static identification/root-n inference for functionals once the right bridge or debiasing object exists, and the rank-test literature targets rank of an estimated matrix. The banked quotient run was rejected precisely because the static null-annihilator criterion was already known, leaving the estimated-operator/estimated-loading local-to-nullspace phase as the explicit unresolved survivor.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to plug the Zhang-Li-Miao-Tchetgen Tchetgen population null-annihilator criterion into a standard delta method fails because the cited weak-functional and rank-test sources point to nonstandard behavior at estimated singular/nullspace boundaries while the prior bank flags near-nullspace diagnostics as the missing object.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-rank asymptotic theorem for a plug-in distance, angle, or quotient diagnostic, with separated Gaussian and n^{-1/2}-local projected-Gaussian or chi-bar-square regimes plus a size-distortion statement for naive quotient decisions.

open_problems.open_problem: How should confidence decisions be calibrated after a proximal quotient diagnostic is used to pretest target uniqueness before reporting Gaussian bridge-functional intervals?

source_refs.claim: Standard estimators, tests, and confidence sets can have nonstandard behavior under weak, semi-strong, and strong identification along drifting sequences.
source_refs.author: Andrews, Cheng

source_refs.claim: GMM confidence procedures require care when parameters are unidentified or weakly identified in parts of the parameter space.
source_refs.author: Andrews, Cheng

source_refs.claim: Extremum estimators and Wald statistics have drifting-sequence behavior when Jacobians move from deficient rank to strong identification.
source_refs.author: Han, McCloskey

source_refs.excerpt: Gaussian-projection critical value defined in Section 6
rank, nuisance, and weak/null-space cases determining it are not pinned

open_problems.why_unsolved: Weak-identification papers document why pretesting and ordinary Wald inference can fail, but they do not specialize the failure to proximal bridge quotient decisions where the classifier is a function of an estimated bridge nullspace and an estimated causal loading. The prior projection-confidence proposal named the relevant statistic but reviewer evidence says the rank, nuisance, and weak/nullspace cases were not pinned down.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to pretest uniqueness and then conditionally report the usual Gaussian interval fails because Andrews-Cheng style drifting-sequence theory and the banked reviewer note both identify weak/nullspace cases where the reference law changes rather than merely losing power.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A uniformly valid post-diagnostic confidence procedure for the proximal target or diagnostic, with explicit critical values across separated, local, and null regimes and a theorem showing nonvanishing size distortion of the naive pretest-then-Wald rule.

open_problems.open_problem: Which target-relevant singular-value or source-condition boundary separates root-n normal proximal functionals from local quotient phases in finite negative-control bridge models?

source_refs.claim: Source-condition doubly robust inference gives asymptotic normality for linear inverse-problem functionals when either primal or dual problem is sufficiently well posed.
source_refs.author: Bennett, Kallus, Mao, Newey, Syrgkanis, Uehara

source_refs.claim: Strong functional identification can coexist with weak or nonunique nuisance bridge functions, including proximal causal inference examples.
source_refs.author: Bennett, Kallus, Mao, Newey, Syrgkanis, Uehara

source_refs.claim: Root-n efficient inference without bridge uniqueness is possible under additional regularity, motivating a boundary question rather than a uniqueness threshold.
source_refs.author: Zhang, Li, Miao, Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.excerpt: rho_n(P_n) is the sharp proximal identification-strength variable
not a local triangular-array rho_n weak-proxy phase diagram

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing source-condition and strongly-identified-functional papers provide positive normality results, while the prior completeness run says the missing novelty is a local triangular-array phase diagram indexed by a target-relevant strength variable. What remains unresolved is the boundary behavior when the target loading approaches the bridge nullspace at the same scale as sampling noise.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to reuse the smallest singular value or invoke an existing source condition fails because the reviewer evidence says the phase variable must be target-relevant after bridge profiling, while Bennett-type results assume conditions that keep the functional on the normal side of the boundary.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A local asymptotic experiment for finite bridge matrices and loadings, proving strong, weak, and failed-completeness or quotient-local limits and identifying the exact target-relevant strength variable that routes the diagnostic law.

open_problems.open_problem: Which minimal 2-by-2 or 3-by-3 negative-control matrices separate unique bridge, nonunique bridge with quotient-identified target, and non-singleton target phases under local perturbations?

source_refs.claim: Double negative-control bridge models give finite negative-control structures for identifying causal effects, but do not classify local quotient-inference witnesses.
source_refs.author: Miao, Shi, Li, Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Categorical negative-control inference supplies finite proxy models useful for hand witnesses, while the quotient phase remains a separate target-loading question.
source_refs.author: Shi, Miao, Nelson, Tchetgen Tchetgen
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B

source_refs.claim: When completeness fails, proxy variables can yield partial-identification bounds rather than point identification, separating lack of completeness from quotient-identified targets.
source_refs.author: Ghassami, Shpitser, Tchetgen Tchetgen

open_problems.why_unsolved: Published finite negative-control and partial-identification papers give model classes and estimators, but the prior quotient bank explicitly records the missing small witness taxonomy after rejecting the static quotient criterion as already solved. The unresolved piece is a compact auditable example that isolates target uniqueness from bridge uniqueness and makes the local diagnostic phase visible.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to exhibit any rank-deficient proxy matrix fails because the banked sources require a witness that separates target uniqueness from bridge uniqueness and the published partial-ID sources show that rank or completeness failure alone may only imply bounds.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An explicit matrix classification or counterexample theorem with 2-by-2 or 3-by-3 operators, loadings, and local sampling perturbations exhibiting each phase and supporting the quotient-inference theorem.

open_problems.open_problem: Which algebraic bridge-null directions in finite quotient diagnostics are actually attainable by latent negative-control causal laws satisfying the exclusion restrictions?

source_refs.excerpt: every rational h in H(P) is realized by a finite latent proximal model
bridge-null directions are induced by actual latent negative-control causal models

open_problems.why_unsolved: Prior attempts treated observable bridge fibers as sharp causal directions, but the rejected-angle and failed-quotient bank entries preserve the realizability question as a separate gap: algebraic nullspace arrays need not automatically correspond to a latent law obeying the negative-control restrictions.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to use every bridge-null vector as a local causal perturbation fails because the banked rejected angle asks whether rational bridge fibers are realizable and the failed quotient gap separately demands latent negative-control induction of null directions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite response-type construction or counterexample theorem characterizing exactly when bridge-fiber directions can be completed to latent proximal laws, with consequences for which local quotient diagnostics have causal rather than algebraic content.

literature_map:
Zhang-Li-Miao-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2023 is the closest proximal anchor: nonunique bridges, solution sets, and root-n inference for counterfactual mean functionals.
Cui-Pu-Shi-Miao-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2024 and Ying-Miao-Shi-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2023 establish semiparametric proximal bridge/AIPW or PDR inference under bridge-based identification.
Bennett-Kallus-Mao-Newey-Syrgkanis-Uehara 2023 gives the nearest inverse-problem anchor: strongly identified functionals despite weak or nonunique nuisance bridge functions.
Bennett et al. 2023 source-condition DR inference gives a positive normality route when either primal or dual inverse problem is sufficiently well posed.
Kleibergen-Paap 2006 and Robin-Smith 2000 provide estimated-matrix rank-test machinery, but not target-loading-to-nullspace quotient phases.
Andrews-Cheng 2012/2014 and Han-McCloskey 2019 provide weak/singular identification drifting-sequence evidence for nonstandard Wald and pretest behavior.
Drton 2009 and Andrews 1999 supply tangent-cone and boundary asymptotic geometry relevant to projected-Gaussian or chi-bar-square laws.
Miao-Shi-Li-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2024 and Shi-Miao-Nelson-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2020 provide finite negative-control bridge/categorical models useful for small matrix witnesses.
Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2023 treats proxy partial identification when completeness fails, separating lack of completeness from quotient-identified targets.
The live gap is not bridge existence, completeness, the Zhang static nullspace iff, or generic weak-IV analogies; it is joint operator/loading estimation and local-to-nullspace diagnostic calibration.
The Stage-6 study-pipeline LATE/Manski/interval-regression gaps were considered as augmentation but did not survive topic-specific G1/G3 filtering for this proximal quotient run.
prior_proposal_map:
Active research/eid_proximal_quotient_inference_phase contains only init/state/pipeline files; no active proposal, reviews, or prior gaps file was available.
Closest direct prior is failed/eid_proximal_bridge_quotient_v1: static quotient criterion rejected as already solved, but suggested directions name estimated-certificate inference and small witnesses.
Mined failed/eid_proximal_completeness_v1: local-to-incomplete rho_n weak-proxy phase and projection-confidence-set conjectures are directly adjacent.
Mined downgraded/eid_proximal_phase_v1: finite bridge-fiber, native-selector, realizability, and two-by-two witness conjectures remain useful while static row-space facts are already-known.
Mined failed/eid_proximal_did_bridge_frontier_v1 for adjacent negative-control finite bridge-rank frontier notes; useful mainly for finite witnesses, not the quotient inference kernel.
Mined DML orthogonality/uniform entries only as weak-inference/studentization shape analogues; they were not retained as standalone final gaps.
Mined pid_bound_intersection_inference for tangent-cone/bootstrap-failure phase language matching the cone-angle aspect; retained only through the quotient-local calibration framing.
No accepted/candidate bank entry appears to solve proximal quotient inference; useful material is mostly failed/downgraded scope flags and reviewer objections.
